contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 180
- Description: Handoff Auto-start feature + /chat session-lock ordering fix
  
  GitHub evidence (PR or commit):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/commit/bbff316
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/commit/ee74954
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/commit/347a8b0
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/commit/43ba734
  
  Details:
  Implemented an opt-in Auto-start marker for handoffs (skip the initial 'go for it' wait for specific plans, per governor discussion): HANDOFF_MANIFEST.md schema + validator (agentic_ai_context, commit bbff316) and the runtime resolver in telegram_adapter.py (truesight_autopilot, commit ee74954), both with new tests. While scoping a governor question about whether pinging an already-busy Sophia thread is safe, found that scenario was already safe (/chat-blocking already locks correctly) but found + fixed a related real bug: /chat (the Telegram adapter's own dispatch endpoint) read/mutated/persisted session history BEFORE acquiring the per-session lock, unlike /chat-blocking (commit 347a8b0) -- the same class of corruption that lock exists to prevent. Hit and fixed a real nonlocal/UnboundLocalError bug along the way, added 3 regression tests. Also caught and fixed a stale monkeypatch in test_degenerate_thread_selfheal.py before pushing (commit 43ba734). All four units merged directly to main per explicit governor direction ('Merge and then deploy') rather than through individual PRs -- no PR URLs exist for this batch, citing commit SHAs as evidence instead, per the governor's direction to use commit URLs when there's no PR (this also prompted a companion fix to this very CLI to accept commit-URL evidence, filed separately once merged). 653/653 tests passing after the final merge; deploy verified via health check + service logs.
- Contributor(s): Claude Anthropic
- TDG Issued: 300.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
